rulings.Case Title: PETER ALLEN VS. WEDRIVEU HOLDINGS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Notice Of Settlement And Motion For An Order For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement [C.C.P. 877.6(A)(2)]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Monday, Mar-30-2026, LINE 1. DEFENDANTS WEDRIVEU, INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS WEDRIVEU HOLDINGS, INC.), and CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Joint Notice Of Settlement And Motion For An Order For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement [C.C.P. 877.6(A)(2)]. The unopposed motion for a determination that the settlement agreement entered into by and between defendants WEDRIVEU, INC. and City and County of San Francisco, and Plaintiff Peter Allen was in good faith pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 877.6. All present and future claims and cross-complaints against WEDRIVEU, INC. and the City and County of San Francisco for indemnity, apportionment of fault and contribution arising out of this case are therefore dismissed and barred. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SWISH ANALYTICS, INC. VS. ODDSJAM, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony Of Defendants Person Most Qualified And Production Of Documents And For Sanctions [Redacted]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Monday, Mar-30-2026, LINE 2. 2-PLAINTIFF SWISH ANALYTICS, INC.'S, A DELAWARE CORPORATION, Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony Of Defendants Person Most Qualified And Production Of Documents And For Sanctions [Redacted]. Hearing required. The court is inclined to continue this matter for hearing and conduct an informal discovery conference with the parties in the interim. Although the motion is nominally about Swish's PMQ notice, Swish's primary concern appears to be document production in light of OddsJam's post-motion offer to produce its PMQ again. In its PMQ motion, OddsJam makes similar allegations that Swish has not produced emails and other documents. If the parties can be ready on March 30 during the noticed hearing time to discuss objections and a production schedule for all of the document requests referenced in their respective moving papers, the court will dedicate time for that at the conclusion of the calendar. The court asks each party to bring a concise chart showing the categories of documents it believes is outstanding and the status of its own collection and production efforts. Alternatively, the IDC can be set at the parties' convenience in the next couple of weeks. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: SWISH ANALYTICS, INC. VS. ODDSJAM, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Person Most Qualified And Production Of Documents And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Monday, Mar-30-2026, LINE 2. 1-DEFENDANTS ODDSJAM, INC., and OPTICODDS, INC.'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Person Most Qualified And Production Of Documents And Sanctions. Defendants OddsJam, Inc. and OpticOdds Inc.'s (collectively OddsJam) motion to compel further deposition of Plaintiff Swish Analytics, Inc.'s person most qualified is continued for hearing to May 28, 2026. OddsJam failed to adequately meet and confer before filing this motion. Swish offered to meet and confer on February 27, 2026, but OddsJam insisted on filing this motion on February 24, 2026. (Fisher Dec., paras. 11-12.) OddsJam offers no reason why the motion needed to be filed that day. Relying on the other party's statement that its PMQ was prepared, without waiting for a meet-and-confer session, is an inadequate effort to informally resolve a discovery dispute through good faith efforts before invoking the court's intervention. To the extent OddsJam seeks an order compelling production of documents, its motion is defective because it does not include a separate statement concerning the RFPs it moves on. The court's denial of this motion is premised on Swish's agreement, made during the parties' February 27 meet and confer conference, that it would provide further PMQ testimony and continue its document production efforts. It makes more sense in this case for PMQ testimony to occur after production of ESI, and the court expects Swish to provide further testimony on the PMQ topics after production has occurred, in conjunction with individual depositions. OddsJam may make a further motion if that does not occur. The court orders OddsJam to pay $3000 as partial sanctions to Swish within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LEE GORDON BLUCKER ET AL VS. MIRABELLE HWANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For A Protective Order, Defendant Mirabelle Hwang'S Motion For A Protective Order, Declaration Of Steven Sobieraj
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Monday, Mar-30-2026, LINE 3. DEFENDANT MIRABELLE HWANG'S Motion For A Protective Order. Hearing required. The court's indicated appears below. Defendant Mirabelle Hwang's motion for protective order is granted in part and continued in part. The records plaintiff seeks are probative. However, although plaintiff seeks only records of whether a cell phone was used at the time of the incident or not, and not the content of the records, in the court's experience large institutional record custodians often do not redact for protection of privacy or limit their production to responsive documents. The court orders that the records be produced directly to the court and will set a further hearing date for release of responsive records, if any. A hearing is required to set further dates. No sanctions are ordered; both parties acted with substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: ALEX ANDRADE ET AL VS. KNOX SRO LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Dismissing The Action Of Plaintiffs Robert Harrison, Rebecca Hoots, Henry Howard, And Angel Liu, And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Monday, Mar-30-2026, LINE 4. DEFENDANTS TENANTS AND OWNERS DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION, JOHN STEWART COMPANY, and THE KNOX PARTNERS LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S Motion For Order Dismissing The Action Of Plaintiffs Robert Harrison, Rebecca Hoots, Henry Howard, And Angel Liu, And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions. The motion to dismiss plaintiffs Rebecca Hoots, Henry Howard, and Angel Liu as a terminating sanction is granted. Defendants show that these plaintiffs have not complied with the court's order to provide verified discovery. Plaintiffs opposed in a declaration stating they could explain in camera. The court denied the request, explaining that on a discovery motion it cannot consider reasons not provided to the opposing party, and continued the matter for hearing so the plaintiffs could file a supplemental opposition in the public record. Plaintiffs have not provided any further opposition. The court concludes that these plaintiffs have effectively abandoned the case and that dismissal is warranted to avoid prejudice to defendants, who cannot take discovery from absent plaintiffs. rulings.Case Title: JOAN CRUZ VS. LYFT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer For Forum Non-Conveniens; Declaraiton Of Gregory G. Brezovec
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Monday, Mar-30-2026, LINE 7. DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S Motion To Transfer For Forum Non-Conveniens. Defendant Lyft, Inc.'s motion to transfer venue to Riverside County based upon the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice is denied without prejudice. First, not all defendants have answered. (See Cholakian & Associates v. Superior Court (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 361, 372 ["both the purpose of section 396b, subdivision (d) and the policy relating to venue favor interpreting that subdivision to require that answers of all proper defendants be filed before an opposition to a motion to transfer to a correct court will be considered on the basis of convenience of witnesses or the ends of justice"].) Second, Defendant fails to maintain its evidentiary burden. Defendant notes that co-defendant Hsieng Liao (driver) resides in Riverside County and plaintiff resides in San Bernadino County. But absent extraordinary circumstances, the court only considers the convenience of the nonparty witnesses. (Winn v. Ohlandt (1931) 213 Cal. 158, 160; Chaffin Const. Co. v. Maleville Bros. (1957) 155 Cal.App.2d 660, 663.) Defendant must present competent evidence and not mere conclusions to meet its burden. (See Tutor-Saliba-Perini Joint Venture v. Superior Court (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 736, 744 [motion to be supported by "competent evidence"]; Lieppman v. Lieber (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 914, 919 [declarations based on information and belief and containing generalities are insufficient to change venue based on inconvenience of witnesses].) Here, defendant exclusively relies on counsel's vague declaration, which is largely based on information and belief. (Brezovec Decl., par. 7.) Counsel does not show personal knowledge of where witnesses and Liao reside. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
